topics.title: 9/11 Anniversary Commemoration and Studio Stories
topics.summary: The show features a discussion regarding the upcoming 9/11 anniversary, reflections on the search for Osama bin Laden, and a recount of an incident where Fred Durst was injured during a bachelor party.

topics.title: 9/11 Anniversary Tributes and Football Pool Results
topics.summary: The studio reflects on the anniversary of a 9/11 broadcast, discusses the results of a football pool, reports on the death of Anna Nicole Smith's son, and reacts to a song by Mark Harris.

topics.title: Mike Tyson Press Conference and Show Highlights
topics.summary: The studio reviews a compilation of humorous clips, including Mike Tyson's aggressive press conference behavior and various recurring segments from the show.

topics.title: Promotional Plans and Celebrity Sightings
topics.summary: The studio discusses upcoming film premieres, internal production meetings, and various celebrity anecdotes involving Jim Carrey and Renee Zellweger.

topics.title: Cleveland Tour Anecdotes and Premiere Plans
topics.summary: The studio features a discussion regarding past experiences with women during a tour in Cleveland, the logistics of an upcoming premiere, and personal dating boundaries.

topics.title: Radio Show Production and Guest Commentary
topics.summary: The studio discusses upcoming segments, including a 9/11 anniversary special, internal production meetings, and listener feedback regarding recent interviews with Eddie Van Halen and Steve O.

topics.title: Media Commentary and 9/11 Anniversary Reflections
topics.summary: The studio discusses various topics including celebrity rumors, a local news anchor's health, and the decision to broadcast historical audio from the September 11 attacks.

topics.title: Post-9/11 Reflections and Comedy Bits
topics.summary: The studio discusses the geopolitical aftermath of 9/11, fluctuating gas prices before an election, and various listener-submitted comedy bits.

topics.title: September 11th Anniversary Commemoration
topics.summary: The studio prepares to broadcast a historical recording of the radio show from September 11, 2001, while discussing various topics including local news clips and conspiracy theories.

So at 8/30, now that I think about it, it's the right thing to do. I saw that CNN all day is going to broadcast

Yeah. And I don't know if I'd want to watch that, but...

So the show will be from 6 a.m. to 8/30, and then at 8/30, we will begin the replay of the 9/11 events
as they unfolded that morning five years ago.
I guess the fifth anniversary is a big deal.
It is. And I find every time I see a mention of 9/11 or I read about the people who died there,

To his bowling life.

Okay.

He probably knows.

So I don't think he would be that insane.

And the only explanation I have for that is we've become, let's say, not focused on the mission because we're spread too thin.
And secondly, I think that this is a different situation than Saddam Hussein.
There were enough people in Iraq who hated Saddam Hussein that we could find him and they would squeal on him.
In this situation, he is very loved of Osama bin Laden.
He is beloved by and feared.
But we were also occupying the country that Saddam was in.
That's right.
It was a different situation.

But he's in a difficult situation, too.
We're not sure who's running that country.

That would be the end.
